nodes.name: coding-style.md
nodes.summary: Prescribes web coding style conventions for the ECC project, covering file organization, CSS custom properties, animation-only properties, semantic HTML usage, and naming conventions.

nodes.name: design-quality.md
nodes.summary: Defines web design quality standards enforced by ECC, including an anti-template policy with banned patterns, required qualities, worthwhile style directions, and a component checklist for pre-flight review.

nodes.name: hooks.md
nodes.summary: Specifies recommended Claude Code hook configurations for web projects, including PostToolUse hooks for format-on-save, linting, type checking, and CSS linting, plus PreToolUse file-size guards and a Stop hook for final build verification.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides complete JSON hook configuration snippets for settings.json, covering the full format/lint/type-check/css-lint/file-guard/build-verify lifecycle.

nodes.name: patterns.md
nodes.summary: Documents recommended web component and data patterns for ECC projects, covering compound components, render props/slots, container/presentational split, state management, URL-as-state, and data fetching strategies including stale-while-revalidate, optimistic updates, and parallel loading.

nodes.name: performance.md
nodes.summary: Sets mandatory web performance rules for ECC projects, specifying Core Web Vitals targets, bundle budgets, loading strategies, image optimization, font loading, animation performance, and a performance checklist.

nodes.name: security.md
nodes.summary: Establishes mandatory web security rules for ECC projects, covering Content Security Policy with nonce-based CSP, XSS prevention, third-party script controls, HTTPS and security headers, and form security requirements.

nodes.name: testing.md
nodes.summary: Defines web testing rules and priority order for ECC projects, prescribing visual regression, accessibility, performance, cross-browser, and responsive testing as the mandatory test hierarchy, along with E2E shape guidelines and unit test conventions.
